records.ety: [L. eludere, elusum; e + ludere to play: cf. F. luder. See Ludicrous.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Eluded; p. pr. & vb. n. Eluding.]

defs: To avoid slyly, by artifice, stratagem, or dexterity; to escape from in a covert manner; to mock by an unexpected escape; to baffle; as, to elude an officer; to elude detection, inquiry, search, comprehension; to elude the force of an argument or a blow.
quotes: Me gentle Delia beckons from the plain, Then, hid in shades, eludes he eager swain.
The transition from fetichism to polytheism seems a gradual process of which the stages elude close definition.
